channel.title: Stockwatch - Key Events
channel.description: Real-time corporate announcements, deals, and financial results.

item.title: [HDFCLIFE] HDFC Life: FY26 Earnings Call Transcript Released
item.description:
HDFC Life Insurance Company Ltd has released the transcript of its earnings conference call held on April 16, 2026, to discuss the financial performance for the year ended March 31, 2026. The company maintained its position among the top three insurers by individual WRP, with a private sector market share of 15.2% for 11 months FY26. Retail protection grew 43%, and the agency channel also grew ahead of the sector. While FY26 top-line was below original expectations, with a Q4 slowdown due to unabsorbed GST, bancassurance softness, and demand deferment, proprietary channels delivered healthy growth of 15-16%. Individual APE growth for the full year was 7% year-on-year. The company insured over 46 million lives, with 70% new customers being first-time buyers. Product mix included ULIP at 44%, non-par savings at 18%, participating products at 25%, term at 7%, and annuity at 5%. Protection was a key highlight, expanding its retail mix to 7.2%.

  • HDFC Life released FY26 earnings call transcript for investors.
  • FY26 individual APE grew 7% YoY, below original expectations.
  • Retail protection grew 43%, with mix expanding to 7.2%.
  • Q4 slowdown attributed to GST, bancassurance, and global uncertainty.
  • Proprietary channels showed strong 15-16% growth in Q4 and FY26.

📄 Read Official Filing (PDF)


Company: HDFC Life Insurance Company Ltd | Category: Quarterly Result

item.title: [HDFCLIFE] HDFC Life: HDFC Bank's stake to rise to 50.27% post preferential issue
item.description:
HDFC Life Insurance Company Limited announced an update on its proposed preferential issue of 1,45,23,906 equity shares to its promoter, HDFC Bank Limited. Following this private placement, HDFC Bank's shareholding in HDFC Life will increase to 50.27% on a fully diluted basis. The total number of fully diluted equity shares of the company post-issue, as of April 16, 2026, will be 2,18,39,50,540. This disclosure was made based on a suggestion from the Stock Exchanges regarding the company's in-principle application for the preferential issue.

  • HDFC Life to issue 1.45 Cr equity shares to HDFC Bank via preferential placement.
  • HDFC Bank's stake in HDFC Life will increase to 50.27% post-issue.
  • Total fully diluted equity shares will be 2,18,39,50,540 after the issue.
  • The move strengthens HDFC Bank's control over its insurance arm.

📄 Read Official Filing (PDF)


Company: HDFC Life Insurance Company Ltd | Category: Shareholding

item.title: [CHOICEIN] Choice Intl: Monitoring Agency confirms no deviation in warrant use
item.description:
Choice International Ltd has submitted the Monitoring Agency Report for the quarter ended March 31, 2026, issued by India Ratings and Research Private Limited. The report monitors the utilization of proceeds from a preferential issue of share warrants. It confirms that all share warrants have been fully converted into equity shares as of March 31, 2026, with no outstanding warrants or unutilized proceeds remaining. The report explicitly states that there has been no deviation in the use of proceeds from the objects stated in the Explanatory Statement to the EGM notice and corrigendum. This confirmation is based on management undertaking and a Statutory Auditor Certificate dated April 23, 2026.

  • Monitoring Agency Report for Q4 FY26 submitted for warrant proceeds.
  • No deviation found in the utilization of preferential issue proceeds.
  • All share warrants fully converted into equity shares by March 31, 2026.
  • No outstanding warrants or unutilized proceeds remain.
  • Report based on management undertaking and statutory auditor certificate.

📄 Read Official Filing (PDF)


Company: CHOICE INTERNATIONAL LTD. | Category: Corporate Action

item.title: [NIITMTS] NIIT Learning Systems files appeal against IT order for AY 2023-24
item.description:
NIIT Learning Systems Ltd provided an update regarding an Income Tax Department order for Assessment Year 2023-24. The initial order, issued under Section 143(3) with a demand notice under Section 156, contained apparent mistakes in the computation sheet, including a short credit of prepaid taxes. In response, the company filed an application for rectification under Section 154 of the Income-tax Act, 1961, on April 23, 2026. Additionally, the company filed an appeal before the Commissioner of Income Tax (Appeals) [CIT(A)] on the same date, within the stipulated time limit, challenging the aforesaid order.

  • Company received an Income Tax order for AY 2023-24.
  • Order contained computation errors and short credit of prepaid taxes.
  • NIIT Learning Systems filed a rectification application on April 23, 2026.
  • An appeal was also filed with CIT(A) on April 23, 2026.
  • All actions were taken within prescribed time limits.

📄 Read Official Filing (PDF)


Company: NIIT Learning Systems Ltd | Category: Tax & Penalty

item.title: [BALRAMCHIN*] Balrampur Chini Mills: ₹450 Cr Preferential Issue, ₹200 Cr NCDs, PLA CAPEX Revised
item.description:
Balrampur Chini Mills' Board approved a preferential issue of 93.17 lakh equity shares at ₹483 each, raising ₹450 crore from promoters and non-promoters. The company also approved issuing secured Non-Convertible Debentures (NCDs) worth ₹200 crore via private placement. Additionally, the estimated capital outlay for the 80,000 TPA Poly Lactic Acid (PLA) Project was revised upwards by ₹230 crore to ₹3,080 crore, primarily due to cost escalations and design changes. An Extra-Ordinary General Meeting (EGM) will be held on May 20, 2026, to seek shareholder approval for the preferential issue.

  • Board approved a preferential issue of 93.17 lakh equity shares at ₹483 each, raising ₹450 crore.
  • Company to issue secured Non-Convertible Debentures (NCDs) worth ₹200 crore via private placement.
  • CAPEX for the 80,000 TPA Poly Lactic Acid (PLA) Project increased by ₹230 crore to ₹3,080 crore.
  • Shareholder approval for the preferential issue will be sought at an EGM on May 20, 2026.

📄 Read Official Filing (PDF)


Company: BALRAMPUR CHINI MILLS LTD. | Category: Corporate Action

item.title: [ORIENTCEM] Orient Cement launches 'Saksham Niveshak' for unclaimed dividends
item.description:
Orient Cement Limited has initiated the "Second 100 Days Campaign - Saksham Niveshak" from April 1, 2026, to July 9, 2026. This campaign, requested by the Investor's Education and Protection Fund Authority (IEPFA), Ministry of Corporate Affairs, aims to assist shareholders in claiming their unpaid or unclaimed dividends. The company is urging shareholders to update their KYC details, including PAN, nomination, contact information, bank account details, and specimen signatures, with the company or its Registrar & Transfer Agent, KFin Technologies Limited. This is crucial as dividends are paid electronically. The initiative seeks to prevent the transfer of these dividends to the IEPF. Orient Cement will send out communications and has made details of unpaid dividends for the past seven years available on its website.

  • Company launched "Saksham Niveshak" campaign for unclaimed dividends.
  • Campaign runs from April 1, 2026, to July 9, 2026.
  • Shareholders must update KYC details to claim unpaid dividends.
  • Aims to prevent dividend transfer to IEPF.
  • Forms and details are available on company and RTA websites.

📄 Read Official Filing (PDF)


Company: Orient Cement Ltd | Category: Dividend

item.title: [HEXT] Hexaware: Shawn Devilla resigns as Non-Executive Director
item.description:
Hexaware Technologies announced the resignation of Mr. Shawn Devilla as a Non-Executive Non-Independent Director, effective April 22, 2026. The resignation is attributed to new professional commitments following his transition from The Carlyle Group. As a result, Mr. Devilla will also cease to be a member of the Stakeholders Relationship Committee and Risk Management Committee of the Board. The company has made this disclosure in compliance with Regulation 30 of the SEBI (Listing Obligations and Disclosure Requirements) Regulations, 2015.

  • Shawn Devilla resigned as Non-Executive Non-Independent Director.
  • Resignation is effective from April 22, 2026.
  • Reason for resignation cited as new professional commitments.
  • Ceased membership in Stakeholders Relationship and Risk Management Committees.

📄 Read Official Filing (PDF)


Company: Hexaware Technologies Ltd | Category: Management Change

item.title: [BALRAMCHIN*] Balrampur Chini to Raise ₹650 Cr via Preferential Issue & NCDs; PLA CAPEX up to ₹3,080 Cr
item.description:
Balrampur Chini Mills Ltd's Board approved raising up to ₹450 crore through a preferential issue of 93.16 lakh equity shares at ₹483 each to promoters, promoter group, and non-promoters. Additionally, the company will raise up to ₹200 crore via private placement of Listed, Secured, Non-Convertible Debentures (NCDs). The Board also revised the estimated capital outlay for its 80,000 TPA Poly Lactic Acid (PLA) project from ₹2,850 crore to ₹3,080 crore, an increase of ₹230 crore. This revision is primarily due to cost escalations in construction materials, global supply chain disruptions, and design changes. An Extra-Ordinary General Meeting (EGM) is scheduled for May 20, 2026, to seek shareholder approval for the preferential issue.

  • Company to raise ₹450 Cr via preferential issue of 9.31 Mn equity shares at ₹483 each.
  • Promoters, promoter group, and non-promoters will participate in the preferential issue.
  • Board approved raising up to ₹200 Cr via private placement of Non-Convertible Debentures.
  • CAPEX for the 80,000 TPA PLA project revised to ₹3,080 Cr from ₹2,850 Cr.
  • Project cost increased by ₹230 Cr due to material price hikes and supply chain issues.

📄 Read Official Filing (PDF)


Company: BALRAMPUR CHINI MILLS LTD. | Category: Corporate Action

item.title: [BALRAMCHIN*] BCML revises PLA project CAPEX to ₹3,080 Cr, raises ₹650 Cr
item.description:
🔥 Trending

Balrampur Chini Mills Ltd (BCML) announced a significant capital raise and project cost revision. The Board approved a preferential issue of up to 93,16,771 equity shares at ₹483 each, totaling ₹450 crore, to promoters, promoter group, and non-promoters. This is subject to shareholder approval at an EGM on May 20, 2026. Additionally, the company plans to issue Listed, Secured, Non-Convertible Debentures (NCDs) aggregating up to ₹200 crore on a private placement basis. The estimated capital outlay for the 80,000 TPA Poly Lactic Acid (PLA) Project has been revised upwards from ₹2,850 crore to ₹3,080 crore, an increase of ₹230 crore. This revision is primarily due to cost escalations in construction materials, global supply chain disruptions, and engineering/design changes.

  • BCML to raise ₹450 crore via preferential issue of 9.31 million equity shares at ₹483 each.
  • Company also plans to issue Listed, Secured, Non-Convertible Debentures worth ₹200 crore.
  • PLA Project CAPEX revised to ₹3,080 crore from ₹2,850 crore, an increase of ₹230 crore.
  • Cost escalation driven by higher construction material prices and supply chain issues.
  • Shareholder approval for preferential issue to be sought at May 20, 2026 EGM.

📄 Read Official Filing (PDF)


Company: BALRAMPUR CHINI MILLS LTD. | Category: Corporate Action

item.title: [SCANSTL] Scan Steels Board to Allot 21,44,239 Equity Shares from OCRPS Conversion
item.description:
Scan Steels Ltd has announced that its Board of Directors will convene a meeting on Monday, April 27, 2026, at 4:00 P.M. The primary agenda for this meeting is to consider and approve the allotment of 21,44,239 equity shares. These shares will be issued upon the conversion of existing Optionally Convertible Redeemable Preference Shares (OCRPS) that were previously allotted on a preferential basis. The conversion and subsequent allotment will adhere to the terms of issue and relevant provisions of the Companies Act, 2013, and SEBI regulations.

  • Board meeting scheduled for April 27, 2026.
  • To approve allotment of 21,44,239 equity shares.
  • Shares result from conversion of Optionally Convertible Redeemable Preference Shares.
  • Allotment will comply with Companies Act and SEBI regulations.

📄 Read Official Filing (PDF)


Company: Scan Steels Limited | Category: Board Meeting
